assertions.text: A behavior-spec.md is produced capturing method signatures, normal behaviors, edge cases, and quirks
assertions.evidence: behavior-spec.md contains: Public Interface table (8 methods), Normal Behaviors section, Edge Cases section, Quirks section (6 items), Out of Scope section. Comprehensive.

assertions.text: Spec is explicitly presented to user for confirmation before Phase 3 begins
assertions.evidence: The behavior-spec.md file is produced but contains no explicit 'please confirm before I proceed to Phase 3' statement. The skill requires presenting the spec to the user and waiting for approval — this step is missing from the output.

assertions.text: Characterization tests are written to capture actual behavior before any migration code
assertions.evidence: DateUtilsCharacterizationTest.kt was produced (335 lines) with tests against the existing Java implementation. No migration code was written.

assertions.text: Timezone implicit dependency (using default timezone) is identified as a quirk that must be preserved
assertions.evidence: Quirks section: 'isToday and daysBetween timezone sensitivity. Both methods implicitly use TimeZone.getDefault(). The Kotlin migration must use the same system default timezone to preserve behavior.'

assertions.text: SimpleDateFormat lenient parsing behavior is identified as a quirk to be documented
assertions.evidence: SimpleDateFormat lenient parsing (isLenient=true by default, overflows silently) is not mentioned in the spec. The spec mentions ParseException on malformed input but does not document the lenient overflow behavior (e.g., month 13 → January of next year).
